chapter_title.ar: باب فَضْلِ ارْتِبَاطِ الْخَيْلِ وَ إِجْرَائِهَا وَ الرَّمْيِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 22 – Merits of bridling the cavalry horses and injuring them, and the throwing (Archery)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/387)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/387)
text: إِنَّ الْخَيْلَ كَانَتْ وُحُوشاً فِي بِلَادِ الْعَرَبِ فَصَعِدَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ ( عليهما السلام ) عَلَى جَبَلِ جِيَادٍ ثُمَّ صَاحَا أَلَا هَلَا أَلَا هَلْ قَالَ فَمَا بَقِيَ فَرَسٌ إِلَّا أَعْطَاهُمَا بِيَدِهِ وَ أَمْكَنَ مِنْ نَاصِيَتِهِ.

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from someone else, from Aban, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The cavalry horses used to be wild in the Arab countries, so Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> and Ismail<sup>as</sup> ascended upon mount Jiyad, then they both<sup>as</sup> shouted: ‘Do not be loose, do not be loose!’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So there did not remain a horse except that it gave its hand (front leg) to both of them<sup>as</sup>, and enabled its forelock (to be touched by them both<sup>as</sup>)’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from more than one narrator from Aban from Zurarah who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that horses were wild in Arab lands. Once, Ibrahim and Isma’il ‘Alayhima al-Salam, climbed on Jiyad hills. They then called, £ 0 horses come home.’ He (the Imam) then said, ’Every horse then allowed the two of them to touch their legs and forehead.’”
